## claude.ai Supabase
Here are guidelines for using Supabase tools effectively:

- Before making schema changes, use `list_tables` to understand the existing structure
- When debugging issues, start with `get_logs` and `get_advisors` before making changes
- Use `get_project_url` and `get_publishable_api_key` when helping users configure client-side integrations

If you have access to a local development environment with a filesystem and shell:
- Install the Supabase agent skill for critical development and security guidance: `npx skills add supabase/agent-skills` (https://supabase.com/docs/guides/getting-started/ai-skills.md)
- Use the Supabase CLI (`supabase`) for local development workflows such as starting a local stack, managing migrations, and running edge functions locally (https://supabase.com/docs/guides/local-development.md)
- Prefer local development and testing before applying changes to a remote project

If you are running in a web-only or remote environment without filesystem or shell access:
- Rely on the MCP tools directly for all Supabase interactions
- Use `apply_migration` carefully, as changes go directly to the remote project

## nas-direct
This NAS MCP intentionally exposes only a small always-on tool surface:
list_capabilities, get_capability_details, tool_search, invoke_tool, run_command,
check_disk_space, and restart_nas_api.

For any NAS diagnostic, troubleshooting, recovery, storage, Drive/ShareSync,
backup, package, log, filesystem, snapshot, permission, or admin task, call
tool_search first unless the task is exactly disk-space checking, free-form
read-only shell diagnosis, or restarting nas-api. Most NAS capabilities are
hidden from tools/list by design to keep the AI session context small.

list_capabilities browses by group and safety class without invoking anything.
get_capability_details returns one operation's full contract. tool_search returns
clear operation descriptions plus the exact invoke_tool call shape. After choosing
a capability, call invoke_tool with the exact returned name, target, and args. Use
target "edgesynology1", "edgesynology2", or "both".

Write tools require a preview first. To execute after reviewing the preview,
call invoke_tool again with confirmed: true inside args. Do not invent or expose
bearer tokens.

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

file.content:
---
name: project-seafile-nas-mcp-deploy
description: The nas-direct MCP cannot deploy/start the seaf-cli stack; NAS recreate is manual
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: dfa944c2-d48b-4fbd-9288-d3a17154b270
---

The `nas-direct` MCP on edgesynology1 can **not** start, recreate, or `docker compose up`
the seaf-cli containers. Its surface is read-only docker (`ps`/`stats`/`inspect`/`logs`) plus
a fixed set of named Synology service restarts (`restart_network_service_safe`,
`restart_nas_api`, monitor-agent ops). `run_command` blocks all writes, and `docker run` /
`compose up` are explicitly denied as "host-level control". `tool_search` has no seaf-cli
deploy/start/recreate/compose capability.

**Why:** discovered 2026-06-07 while redeploying the seaf-cli stack to ship the new control
dispatcher. `synology-seaf-cli/README.md` still documents deploying via the MCP base64
pattern, but that path is now blocked — treat the README's MCP-deploy steps as stale.

**How to apply:** NAS container restart/recreate must be done by Albert (DSM Container
Manager, or SSH with docker at `/var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker`), or by
adding a deploy capability to the NAS MCP. The VPS `nas-settings` panel, by contrast, IS
rebuildable by Claude directly (Claude runs on the VPS): build from
`/home/ai/seafile-repo/seafile-server` with `docker compose -f nas-settings.yml build
nas-settings`, then recreate from `/opt/seafile` so `/opt/seafile/.env` is loaded. See
[[project_seafile]].

# Synology NAS — Seafile seaf-cli upload containers
# One service per library. Each stages a date-filtered view of a NAS folder
# into /library, then seaf-cli uploads /library to the Seafile server.
#
# === Settings ===
#
# SEAF_INGEST_DAYS     Only upload files modified within this many days.
#                      Set per-library in the environment blocks below.
#                      Remove the line (or leave blank) to upload all files.
#
#   Examples:
#     - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=365    # 1 year
#     - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=730    # 2 years
#     - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=1825   # 5 years
#
# SEAF_UPLOAD_LIMIT    Max upload speed in KB/s (optional).
# SEAF_DOWNLOAD_LIMIT  Max download speed in KB/s (optional).
#   These limit network throughput only, not the CPU hashing phase.
#   Example: 10240 = 10 MB/s
#
# Resource limits:
#   cpu_shares — soft CPU weight (default 1024; 512 = half priority under contention).
#                NOTE: Synology kernel does not support CFS CPU quota (hard cpus limit).
#                cpu_shares only throttles when the NAS is CPU-saturated, not always.
#   memory     — hard limit; OOMKill if exceeded (container restarts cleanly).
#                Tune up if OOMKills appear (check: docker events | grep oom).
#
# =================
#
# Image: ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:seaf-cli-latest
# Wrapper built on flrnnc/seafile-client — adds tini, fixed entrypoint.py.
# Both containers LIVE on edgesynology1 as of 2026-05-08.
# See README.md for deploy instructions.

name: seaf-cli

services:

  seaf-cli-char-licensed:
    image: ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:seaf-cli-latest
    container_name: seaf-cli-char-licensed
    restart: unless-stopped
    cpu_shares: 512
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2g
    volumes:
      - /volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed:/source:ro
      - seaf-cli-char-licensed-staging:/library
      - seaf-cli-char-licensed-data:/seafile
    environment:
      - SEAF_SERVER_URL=https://seafile.designflow.app
      - SEAF_USERNAME
      - SEAF_PASSWORD
      - SEAF_LIBRARY=177cf9de-3066-482e-956a-7ae8d8786c6d
      - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=730
      - SEAF_SOURCE_PATH=/volume1/mac/Decor/Character Licensed
      - SEAF_SETTINGS_URL=https://seafile.designflow.app/nas-settings/api/settings
      - SEAF_STATUS_TOKEN

  seaf-cli-generic-decor:
    image: ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:seaf-cli-latest
    container_name: seaf-cli-generic-decor
    restart: unless-stopped
    cpu_shares: 512
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 512m
    volumes:
      - /volume1/mac/Decor/Generic Decor:/source:ro
      - seaf-cli-generic-decor-staging:/library
      - seaf-cli-generic-decor-data:/seafile
    environment:
      - SEAF_SERVER_URL=https://seafile.designflow.app
      - SEAF_USERNAME
      - SEAF_PASSWORD
      - SEAF_LIBRARY=1b116ab7-d66b-4411-a691-21f34eadb731
      - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=730
      - SEAF_SOURCE_PATH=/volume1/mac/Decor/Generic Decor
      - SEAF_SETTINGS_URL=https://seafile.designflow.app/nas-settings/api/settings
      - SEAF_STATUS_TOKEN

volumes:
  seaf-cli-char-licensed-data:
    name: seaf-cli-char-licensed-data
  seaf-cli-char-licensed-staging:
    name: seaf-cli-char-licensed-staging
  seaf-cli-generic-decor-data:
    name: seaf-cli-generic-decor-data
  seaf-cli-generic-decor-staging:
    name: seaf-cli-generic-decor-staging

content.text:
Great — I have everything I need. Let me address both questions:

---

**Question 2 — Sync speed (answer first, it's quick):**

It's technically **one-way**, not two-way — the NAS pushes files up to Seafile in Brazil; designers can write in Seafile but those changes don't come back to the NAS (the NAS source folder is mounted read-only). From the logs, the hourly cycle looks like this:

| Library | Scan + stage phase | Cycle interval |
|---|---|---|
| Character Licensed (~476K files) | ~1–3 min | every 60 min |
| Generic Decor (~55K files) | ~10–15 sec | every 60 min |

That's just the "get files ready" phase. The actual seaf-cli upload to the S3 bucket in São Paulo happens in the background after "Library ready" and isn't logged separately. If you want to measure upload speed, I can add upload timing to the logs — let me know.

---

**Question 1 — Adding Styleguides:**

The NAS share `/volume1/styleguides` exists and has content (Disney, Marvel, WB, etc.). Seafile has no Styleguides library yet — I need to create one, then add the container to the compose file.

Creating the library now and updating the code:

# CLAUDE.md — Claude Code Instructions

Read **AGENTS.md** first. Everything substantive is there.

---

## Memory

Persistent memory path: `/home/ai/.claude/projects/-/memory/`

---

## Context

Use `.claudeignore` for context economy. It mirrors `.cursorignore`; both are aligned with `AGENTS.md` → What to ignore.

---

## Branch & deployment model

- **`main` only.** Commit straight to `main` — no feature branches, no PRs.
- **CI publishes; it does not deploy.** `.github/workflows/seaf-cli-image.yml` and `nas-settings-image.yml` lint, test, build, and push their images to GHCR. Deployment is a separate manual, repo-driven pull on the target host.
- **SSH is NOT a CI deployment path.** GitHub Actions must never SSH into the VPS/NAS or run Docker there. Claude runs *on* the VPS and may run host commands for manual ops/debugging. NAS container recreates are manual operator SSH work on `edgesynology1`, not NAS MCP work. Production behavior must always be defined by repo files + published images.
- GitHub Secrets hold no deploy/SSH keys — CI uses only `GITHUB_TOKEN`.

---

## Operations Permissions

Claude Code runs on the VPS (`172.233.14.233`) as `ai` (passwordless sudo). You may:
- Run Bash commands on this VPS directly
- Read/write files under `/home/ai/`, `/opt/seafile/` (with sudo), `/opt/seafile-data/` (with sudo)
- Use the `nas-direct` MCP server only if it is available and only for read-only diagnosis on edgesynology1. Base64-encode any read-only docker command because the MCP allowlist blocks the literal string "docker".
- Push to GitHub via `gh` (already authenticated as u2giants)

You may **not**:
- Make changes directly on the VPS or NAS and skip committing them to this repo
- Run `CONFIGURE_OAUTH.sh` or `CREATE_NAS_SYNC_ACCOUNT.sh` — these are one-time scripts already applied
- Enable the Cloudflare proxy on `seafile.designflow.app`

---

## Commit Style

- Short imperative subject line (`fix caddy TLS config`, `add nas-settings panel`)
- No ticket numbers or issue references needed
- Commit meaningful changes together — don't split a compose change from its doc update

---

## Tool Preferences

- Use Read/Edit/Write/Grep over bash equivalents for file operations
- Use `gh` CLI for GitHub operations
- Use SSH, not `nas-direct`, for NAS deploy/recreate work
- Credentials are in `/opt/seafile/CREDENTIALS.txt` (root-only — use sudo) and `/opt/seafile/.env`

# Synology NAS — Seafile seaf-cli upload containers
# One service per library. Each stages a date-filtered view of a NAS folder
# into /library, then seaf-cli uploads /library to the Seafile server.
#
# === Settings ===
#
# SEAF_INGEST_DAYS     Only upload files modified within this many days.
#                      Set per-library in the environment blocks below.
#                      Remove the line (or leave blank) to upload all files.
#
#   Examples:
#     - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=365    # 1 year
#     - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=730    # 2 years
#     - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=1825   # 5 years
#
# SEAF_UPLOAD_LIMIT    Max upload speed in KB/s (optional).
# SEAF_DOWNLOAD_LIMIT  Max download speed in KB/s (optional).
#   These limit network throughput only, not the CPU hashing phase.
#   Example: 10240 = 10 MB/s
#
# Resource limits:
#   cpu_shares — soft CPU weight (default 1024; 512 = half priority under contention).
#                NOTE: Synology kernel does not support CFS CPU quota (hard cpus limit).
#                cpu_shares only throttles when the NAS is CPU-saturated, not always.
#   memory     — hard limit; OOMKill if exceeded (container restarts cleanly).
#                Tune up if OOMKills appear (check: docker events | grep oom).
#
# =================
#
# Image: ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:seaf-cli-latest
# Wrapper built on flrnnc/seafile-client — adds tini, fixed entrypoint.py.
# Both containers LIVE on edgesynology1 as of 2026-05-08.
# See README.md for deploy instructions.

content.text:
Two direct answers:

**1. Separate containers — no, we don't have to.** The current design is one container = one library. I can refactor the entrypoint to accept a list of (source folder → library UUID) pairs and run them all in a single container on the same hourly cycle. That would mean one container total instead of one per folder. Want me to do that?

**2. Sync direction — fair, let me re-ask the actual question.** What did you mean by "2-way sync"? Two possibilities:

- **A)** You just want to know: *how long until a file saved on the NAS shows up in Seafile for the designers?* Answer: worst case ~60 minutes (the hourly cycle) + upload time. We could shorten that by tightening the interval.

- **B)** You want changes designers make *in Seafile* to sync back down to the NAS in NYC. Right now that doesn't happen — the NAS folders are read-only to the containers. If that's what you need, it's a bigger design change.

Which is it?

content.text:
OK, this is important. The current architecture is fundamentally wrong for what you need. Let me be direct about what's broken and what we need to decide before I touch any code.

---

**What's actually broken:**

The current design was built as a one-way batch upload, not a real-time sync engine. Specifically:
- The NAS folders are mounted **read-only** — Seafile changes physically cannot write back to the NAS
- The staging layer exists to filter by date, but it creates a middleman that breaks bidirectional sync entirely
- The hourly cycle is a batch job, not a daemon

For real-time 2-way sync, the design needs to change to: **mount the NAS folders read-write, remove the staging layer, run seaf-cli as a persistent daemon.** seaf-cli actually supports this natively — we've just been misusing it.

---

**One big decision before I start:**

Character Licensed has **476,000+ files** just within the 2-year window. We don't know the total. If we remove the date filter and sync everything bidirectionally, the initial sync could take many hours or days. After that initial catch-up, incremental changes would be near-instant.

Two questions:
1. How far back do the designers actually need to go? (2 years? Everything? Just recent?)
2. Is there a concern about seaf-cli writing metadata files (like `.seafile-data`) into the NAS folders alongside your actual files?

Once you answer those, I can redesign both things at once — real-time 2-way sync + single container for all folders.

385	    def _run_seaf(self, args: list[str], timeout: int = 120) -> tuple[bool, str]:
386	        """Run a seaf-cli subcommand; return (ok, combined output) with any
387	        credentials redacted so they never reach the server's result log."""
388	        try:
389	            proc = subprocess.run(
390	                self.binary + args, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=timeout
391	            )
392	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
393	            return False, "command timed out"
394	        except Exception as exc:
395	            return False, str(exc)
396	        out = ((proc.stdout or "") + (proc.stderr or "")).strip()
397	        for secret in {s for s in (self.password, self.token) if s}:
398	            out = out.replace(secret, "***")
399	        return proc.returncode == 0, out

405	            ok, out = self._run_seaf(["config", "-k", key], timeout=10)
406	            cache[key] = out if (ok and out) else None
407	        self._config_cache = cache

415	    def _save_folder_size_cache(self, cache: dict) -> None:
416	        self.folder_size_cache_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
417	        tmp = self.folder_size_cache_path.with_suffix(".tmp")
418	        tmp.write_text(json.dumps(cache, indent=2, sort_keys=True))

421	    def _scan_tree_size(self, root: Path) -> dict:
422	        total_bytes = 0
423	        file_count = 0
424	        folder_count = 0
425	        errors = 0
426	        stack = [root]
427	        while stack:
428	            current = stack.pop()
429	            try:
430	                with os.scandir(current) as entries:
431	                    for entry in entries:
432	                        try:
433	                            if entry.is_dir(follow_symlinks=False):
434	                                folder_count += 1
435	                                stack.append(Path(entry.path))
436	                            elif entry.is_file(follow_symlinks=False):
437	                                file_count += 1
438	                                total_bytes += entry.stat(follow_symlinks=False).st_size
439	                        except OSError:
440	                            errors += 1
441	            except OSError:
442	                errors += 1
443	        return {
444	            "bytes": total_bytes,
445	            "files": file_count,
446	            "folders": folder_count,
447	            "errors": errors,
448	        }

450	    def _build_folder_size_cache(self) -> dict:
451	        source = self.source
452	        started_at = datetime.datetime.utcnow()
453	        cache = {
454	            "source_path": str(source),
455	            "source_label": os.environ.get("SEAF_SOURCE_PATH", str(source)),
456	            "started_at": started_at.isoformat() + "Z",
457	            "finished_at": None,
458	            "status": "running",
459	            "root": {"bytes": 0, "files": 0, "folders": 0, "errors": 0},
460	            "children": [],
461	        }
462	        self._save_folder_size_cache(cache)
463	
464	        children = []
465	        if source.exists():
466	            entries = []
467	            try:
468	                with os.scandir(source) as scan:
469	                    for entry in scan:
470	                        try:
471	                            entries.append({
472	                                "name": entry.name,
473	                                "path": entry.path,
474	                                "is_dir": entry.is_dir(follow_symlinks=False),
475	                                "is_file": entry.is_file(follow_symlinks=False),
476	                                "size": entry.stat(follow_symlinks=False).st_size
477	                                if entry.is_file(follow_symlinks=False) else 0,
478	                            })
479	                        except OSError:
480	                            entries.append({
481	                                "name": entry.name,
482	                                "path": entry.path,
483	                                "is_dir": False,
484	                                "is_file": False,
485	                                "size": 0,
486	                                "errors": 1,
487	                            })
488	                entries.sort(key=lambda e: e["name"].lower())
489	            except OSError:
490	                entries = []
491	            for entry in entries:
492	                item = {
493	                    "name": entry["name"],
494	                    "path": entry["path"],
495	                    "type": "folder",
496	                    "bytes": 0,
497	                    "files": 0,
498	                    "folders": 0,
499	                    "errors": entry.get("errors", 0),
500	                }
501	                try:
502	                    if entry["is_dir"]:
503	                        item.update(self._scan_tree_size(Path(entry["path"])))
504	                        item["type"] = "folder"
505	                        item["folders"] += 1
506	                    elif entry["is_file"]:
507	                        item.update({"type": "file", "bytes": entry["size"], "files": 1})
508	                    else:
509	                        continue
510	                except OSError:
511	                    item["errors"] = 1
512	                children.append(item)
513	                for key in ("bytes", "files", "folders", "errors"):
514	                    cache["root"][key] += item.get(key, 0)
515	                cache["children"] = children
516	                cache["updated_child"] = entry["name"]
517	                self._save_folder_size_cache(cache)
518	
519	        cache["children"] = sorted(children, key=lambda x: (-x.get("bytes", 0), x.get("name", "").lower()))
520	        cache["finished_at"] = datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z"
521	        cache["status"] = "complete"
522	        cache.pop("updated_child", None)
523	        self._save_folder_size_cache(cache)
524	        return cache

526	    def _refresh_folder_size_cache_async(self, force: bool = False) -> None:
527	        if self._folder_size_scan_running:
528	            return
529	
530	        def _worker() -> None:
531	            with self._folder_size_scan_lock:
532	                self._folder_size_scan_running = True
533	                try:
534	                    logger.info("Scanning cached folder sizes for %s", self.source)
535	                    self._build_folder_size_cache()
536	                    logger.info("Folder-size cache scan complete")
537	                except Exception:
538	                    logger.exception("Folder-size cache scan failed")
539	                finally:
540	                    self._folder_size_scan_running = False
541	
542	        cache = self._load_folder_size_cache()
543	        if not force and cache and cache.get("status") == "complete":
544	            return
545	        self._folder_size_scan_running = True
546	        threading.Thread(target=_worker, daemon=True, name="folder-size-scan").start()

548	    def _start_folder_size_scheduler(self) -> None:
549	        """Refresh recursive folder sizes once per night without blocking sync."""
550	        def _local_now() -> datetime.datetime:
551	            tz = datetime.timezone.utc
552	            if ZoneInfo is not None:
553	                try:
554	                    tz = ZoneInfo(FOLDER_SIZE_SCAN_TZ)
555	                except Exception:
556	                    pass
557	            return datetime.datetime.now(tz)
558	
559	        def _loop() -> None:
560	            last_scan_date = None
561	            cache = self._load_folder_size_cache()
562	            finished_at = cache.get("finished_at") if cache else None
563	            if finished_at:
564	                try:
565	                    last_scan_date = datetime.datetime.fromisoformat(
566	                        finished_at.rstrip("Z")
567	                    ).date()
568	                except Exception:
569	                    pass
570	            while True:
571	                now = _local_now()
572	                if now.hour >= FOLDER_SIZE_SCAN_HOUR and last_scan_date != now.date():
573	                    self._refresh_folder_size_cache_async(force=True)
574	                    last_scan_date = now.date()
575	                time.sleep(300)
576	
577	        threading.Thread(target=_loop, daemon=True, name="folder-size-scheduler").start()
578	        logger.info("Folder-size scheduler started: daily after %02d:00 %s",
579	                    FOLDER_SIZE_SCAN_HOUR, FOLDER_SIZE_SCAN_TZ)
580	
581	    def _schedule_allows_sync(self, schedule: dict | None) -> bool:
582	        if not schedule or not schedule.get("enabled"):
583	            return True
584	
585	        tz = datetime.timezone.utc
586	        if ZoneInfo is not None:
587	            try:
588	                tz = ZoneInfo(schedule.get("timezone") or "UTC")
589	            except Exception:
590	                pass
591	        now = datetime.datetime.now(tz)
592	
593	        def minutes(value: str, fallback: str) -> int:
594	            try:
595	                hour, minute = value.split(":", 1)
596	                return int(hour) * 60 + int(minute)
597	            except Exception:
598	                hour, minute = fallback.split(":", 1)
599	                return int(hour) * 60 + int(minute)
600	
601	        current = now.hour * 60 + now.minute
602	        today = now.weekday()
603	        yesterday = (today - 1) % 7
604	
605	        def window_allows(window: dict) -> bool:
606	            if window.get("enabled") is False:
607	                return False
608	            days = set(window.get("days") or [])
609	            if not days:
610	                return False
611	            start = minutes(window.get("start", "00:00"), "00:00")
612	            end = minutes(window.get("end", "23:59"), "23:59")
613	            if start == end:
614	                return today in days
615	            if start < end:
616	                return today in days and start <= current < end
617	            return (today in days and current >= start) or (yesterday in days and current < end)
618	
619	        windows = schedule.get("windows")
620	        if isinstance(windows, dict):
621	            return any(
622	                window_allows(window)
623	                for window in windows.values()
624	                if isinstance(window, dict)
625	            )
626	
627	        # Backward compatibility for schedules saved before weekday/weekend
628	        # windows were split.
629	        return window_allows({
630	            "enabled": True,
631	            "days": schedule.get("days") or [],
632	            "start": schedule.get("start", "00:00"),
633	            "end": schedule.get("end", "23:59"),
634	        })
635	
636	    def _apply_schedule(self, schedule: dict | None) -> None:
637	        if not hasattr(self, "rpc"):
638	            return
639	        value = "true" if self._schedule_allows_sync(schedule) else "false"
640	        for repo in self.rpc.get_repo_list(-1, -1):
641	            if str(repo.auto_sync).lower() != value:
642	                self.rpc.set_repo_property(repo.id, "auto-sync", value)

644	    def _dispatch_command(self, command: dict) -> dict:
645	        """Execute one queued seaf-cli command and return a result record.
646	
647	        Never raises — the status reporter must keep running no matter what.
648	        """
649	        verb = command.get("verb", "")
650	        args = command.get("args") or {}
651	        result = {"id": command.get("id"), "verb": verb,
652	                  "ok": False, "output": "", "error": "",
653	                  "finished_at": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z"}
654	        try:
655	            if verb in ("pause", "resume"):
656	                # This client's RpcClient has no global enable/disable_auto_sync;
657	                # pausing is per-repo via the "auto-sync" property ("false"/"true").
658	                if not hasattr(self, "rpc"):
659	                    raise RuntimeError("daemon not running")
660	                value = "false" if verb == "pause" else "true"
661	                repos = self.rpc.get_repo_list(-1, -1)
662	                if not repos:
663	                    raise RuntimeError("no synced library yet")
664	                for repo in repos:
665	                    self.rpc.set_repo_property(repo.id, "auto-sync", value)
666	                result.update(
667	                    ok=True,
668	                    output=("paused" if verb == "pause" else "resumed")
669	                    + f" {len(repos)} repo(s)",
670	                )
671	
672	            elif verb in ("restart", "stop"):
673	                # Stop the daemon. watch() then exits and the container's
674	                # restart policy relaunches it (a lasting stop = stop the
675	                # container itself; see the panel's guidance).
676	                self._run_seaf(["stop"], timeout=30)
677	                result.update(ok=True,
678	                              output="daemon stopped; container will relaunch it")
679	
680	            elif verb == "reinit":
681	                # Force a fresh client init on next start: drop the .ini/socket/pid
682	                # (synced data volume is untouched) then stop so the container
683	                # restarts and re-initializes. Libraries are re-cloned.
684	                for stale in (self.ini, self.socket,
685	                              self.seafile / "seafile-data" / "seafile.pid"):
686	                    try:
687	                        stale.unlink()
688	                    except OSError:
689	                        pass
690	                self._run_seaf(["stop"], timeout=30)
691	                result.update(ok=True,
692	                              output="re-initializing; libraries will be re-cloned")
693	
694	            elif verb == "config_get":
695	                key = args.get("key", "")
696	                if not key:
697	                    raise ValueError("missing config key")
698	                ok, out = self._run_seaf(["config", "-k", key], timeout=10)
699	                result.update(ok=ok, output=out)
700	
701	            elif verb == "config_set":
702	                key = args.get("key", "")
703	                value = str(args.get("value", ""))
704	                if not key:
705	                    raise ValueError("missing config key")
706	                ok, out = self._run_seaf(["config", "-k", key, "-v", value], timeout=10)
707	                self._refresh_config_cache()
708	                result.update(ok=ok, output=out or f"{key} = {value}")
709	
710	            elif verb == "list":
711	                ok, out = self._run_seaf(["list"], timeout=30)
712	                result.update(ok=ok, output=out)
713	
714	            elif verb == "list_remote":
715	                ok, out = self._run_seaf(
716	                    ["list-remote", *self._get_credential_args()], timeout=60)
717	                result.update(ok=ok, output=out)
718	
719	            elif verb == "refresh_folder_sizes":
720	                self._refresh_folder_size_cache_async(force=True)
721	                result.update(ok=True, output="folder-size refresh started")
722	
723	            elif verb == "desync":
724	                worktree = args.get("worktree", "")
725	                if not worktree:
726	                    raise ValueError("missing worktree path")
727	                ok, out = self._run_seaf(["desync", "-d", worktree], timeout=30)
728	                result.update(ok=ok, output=out or f"desynced {worktree}")
729	
730	            elif verb == "create":
731	                name = args.get("name", "")
732	                if not name:
733	                    raise ValueError("missing library name")
734	                desc = args.get("desc") or name
735	                cmd = ["create", *self._get_credential_args(), "-n", name, "-t", desc]
736	                if args.get("enc_password"):
737	                    cmd += ["-e", args["enc_password"]]
738	                ok, out = self._run_seaf(cmd, timeout=60)
739	                result.update(ok=ok, output=out)
740	
741	            else:
742	                result["error"] = f"unknown verb: {verb}"
743	        except Exception as exc:
744	            result["error"] = str(exc)
745	
746	        if not result["ok"] and not result["error"]:
747	            result["error"] = result.get("output") or "command failed"
748	        return result

750	    def _start_status_reporter(self, settings_url: str, token: str | None) -> None:
751	        """Start a daemon thread that POSTs sync status every 30 s, carries back
752	        any command results, and executes the next queued command."""
753	        status_url = settings_url.rstrip("/").rsplit("/api/settings", 1)[0] + "/api/status"
754	        self._pending_results: list[dict] = []
755	        self._config_cache = None
756	
757	        def _loop() -> None:
758	            while True:
759	                try:
760	                    payload_obj = self._collect_status()
761	                    if self._pending_results:
762	                        payload_obj["command_results"] = self._pending_results
763	                    payload = json.dumps(payload_obj).encode()
764	                    headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}
765	                    if token:
766	                        headers["X-Status-Token"] = token
767	                    req = urllib.request.Request(
768	                        status_url, data=payload, headers=headers, method="POST"
769	                    )
770	                    with urllib.request.urlopen(req, timeout=10) as resp:
771	                        body = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
772	                    # Results were delivered with this POST — clear them.
773	                    self._pending_results = []
774	                    command = body.get("command")
775	                    if command:
776	                        logger.info("Executing queued command: %s",
777	                                    command.get("verb"))
778	                        self._pending_results.append(self._dispatch_command(command))
779	                    elif "schedule" in body:
780	                        self._apply_schedule(body.get("schedule"))
781	                except Exception:
782	                    pass  # best-effort; never crash the container
783	                time.sleep(STATUS_REPORT_INTERVAL_SECONDS)
784	
785	        threading.Thread(target=_loop, daemon=True, name="status-reporter").start()
786	        logger.info("Status reporter started → %s", status_url)

788	    def healthcheck(self) -> int:
789	        tasks = self.rpc.get_clone_tasks()
790	        healthy = True
791	        for task in tasks:
792	            name = task.repo_name
793	            state = task.state
794	
795	            if state == 'done':
796	                continue
797	            elif state == "fetch":
798	                tx_task = self.rpc.find_transfer_task(task.repo_id)
799	                percentage = 0 if tx_task.block_done == 0 else tx_task.block_done / tx_task.block_total * 100
800	                rate = 0 if tx_task.rate == 0 else tx_task.rate / 1024.0
801	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{percentage:<.1f}%, {rate:<.1f}KB/s")
802	            elif state == "error":
803	                healthy = False
804	                error = self.rpc.sync_error_id_to_str(task.error)
805	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{error}")
806	            else:
807	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}")
808	
809	        repos = self.rpc.get_repo_list(-1, -1)
810	        auto_sync_enabled = self.rpc.is_auto_sync_enabled()
811	        for repo in repos:
812	            name = repo.name
813	
814	            if not auto_sync_enabled or not repo.auto_sync:
815	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{'auto sync disabled':<20s}")
816	                continue
817	
818	            task = self.rpc.get_repo_sync_task(repo.id)
819	            if task is None:
820	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{'waiting for sync':<20s}")
821	                continue
822	
823	            state = task.state
824	            if state in ['uploading', 'downloading']:
825	                tx_task = self.rpc.find_transfer_task(repo.id)
826	                if tx_task.rt_state == "data":
827	                    state += " files"
828	                    percentage = 0 if tx_task.block_done == 0 else tx_task.block_done / tx_task.block_total * 100
829	                    rate = 0 if tx_task.rate == 0 else tx_task.rate / 1024.0
830	                    print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{percentage:<.1f}%, {rate:<.1f}KB/s")
831	                elif tx_task.rt_state == "fs":
832	                    state += " files list"
833	                    percentage = 0 if tx_task.fs_objects_done == 0 else tx_task.fs_objects_done / tx_task.fs_objects_total * 100
834	                    print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{percentage:<.1f}%")
835	            elif state == 'error':
836	                healthy = False
837	                error = self.rpc.sync_error_id_to_str(task.error)
838	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}\t{error}")
839	            else:
840	                print(f"{name:<50s}\t{state:<20s}")
841	
842	        return 0 if healthy else 1

#!/usr/bin/env python3
from __future__ import annotations
"""
seaf-entrypoint.py

Staging wrapper for flrnnc/seafile-client. Hardlinks (or copies, if the source
and staging volume are on different filesystems) the subset of /source modified
within SEAF_INGEST_DAYS days into /library, then hands off to the upstream
entrypoint at /home/seafile/entrypoint.py.

=== Settings (set in docker-compose.yml → environment) ===

  SEAF_INGEST_DAYS   Integer. Only include files modified within this many days.
                     Leave unset or blank to include all files regardless of age.
                     Examples:  365 (1 year)  730 (2 years)  1825 (5 years)

  SEAF_SETTINGS_URL  Optional. URL to a JSON settings endpoint (e.g. the
                     nas-settings panel's /api/settings route). When set, the
                     app fetches per-library ingest_days keyed by SEAF_LIBRARY
                     UUID at startup and on every hourly refresh. On fetch
                     failure it falls back silently to SEAF_INGEST_DAYS.

  SEAF_IGNORE_DIRS   Optional comma-separated directory names to omit from the
                     staged library. Defaults to @eaDir so Synology metadata
                     folders are not uploaded to Seafile.
"""
import datetime
import json
import logging
import os
import shutil
import subprocess
import sys
import threading
import time
import urllib.request
from pathlib import Path

logging.basicConfig(
    level=logging.INFO,
    format='%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(message)s',
)
log = logging.getLogger('seaf-entrypoint')

SOURCE = Path('/source')
LIBRARY = Path('/library')
UPSTREAM = '/home/seafile/entrypoint.py'
DEFAULT_IGNORE_DIRS = {"@eaDir"}


def fetch_ingest_days_from_url(settings_url: str, library_uuid: str, fallback) -> object:
    """
    Fetch per-library ingest_days from the settings API.

    Returns the integer ingest_days value for the matching library, or
    `fallback` if the fetch fails or the library is not found.
    """
    try:
        with urllib.request.urlopen(settings_url, timeout=10) as resp:
            data = json.loads(resp.read().decode())
        for entry in data.values():
            if entry.get("uuid") == library_uuid:
                days = entry.get("ingest_days")
                # None means "all files" — pass through as-is
                log.info(
                    'Settings URL: ingest_days=%s for library %s',
                    days if days is not None else 'all',
                    library_uuid,
                )
                return days
        log.warning('Settings URL: library %s not found in response, using fallback', library_uuid)
    except Exception as exc:
        log.warning('Settings URL fetch failed (%s), using fallback SEAF_INGEST_DAYS', exc)
    return fallback


def resolve_ingest_days(env_days, settings_url: str | None, library_uuid: str | None):
    """
    Return the effective ingest_days value.

    Priority: SEAF_SETTINGS_URL (if reachable and library found) > SEAF_INGEST_DAYS env var.
    """
    if settings_url and library_uuid:
        return fetch_ingest_days_from_url(settings_url, library_uuid, fallback=env_days)
    return env_days


def _same_filesystem(a, b):
    """True if paths a and b live on the same filesystem (hardlinks possible)."""
    try:
        return os.stat(a).st_dev == os.stat(b).st_dev
    except OSError:
        return False


def _place(src, dst, use_links):
    """Materialise src at dst — hardlink when possible, else copy.

    A hardlink shares the source's inode, so it costs ~0 extra disk and is
    near-instant regardless of file size. Falls back to a real copy on any
    error (cross-device link, link-count limits, a FS without hardlinks).
    """
    if use_links:
        try:
            os.link(src, dst)
            return
        except OSError:
            pass
    shutil.copy2(src, dst)


def _ignored_dir_names():
    raw = os.environ.get("SEAF_IGNORE_DIRS")
    if raw is None:
        return DEFAULT_IGNORE_DIRS
    return {name.strip() for name in raw.split(",") if name.strip()}


def scan_source(days, ignored_dirs=None):
    """Return {relpath: mtime} for source files modified within `days` days.

    Uses os.scandir so each file's mtime comes from the directory read itself
    rather than a second stat() syscall per file — roughly halving the metadata
    I/O of the hourly rescan over large trees (Character Licensed ≈ 467k files).
    """
    cutoff = time.time() - days * 86400 if days else 0
    ignored_dirs = DEFAULT_IGNORE_DIRS if ignored_dirs is None else set(ignored_dirs)
    wanted = {}
    stack = [SOURCE]
    while stack:
        current = stack.pop()
        try:
            scanner = os.scandir(current)
        except OSError:
            continue
        with scanner:
            for entry in scanner:
                try:
                    if entry.is_dir(follow_symlinks=False):
                        if entry.name in ignored_dirs:
                            continue
                        stack.append(Path(entry.path))
                    elif entry.is_file(follow_symlinks=False):
                        mtime = entry.stat(follow_symlinks=False).st_mtime
                        if not days or mtime >= cutoff:
                            wanted[Path(entry.path).relative_to(SOURCE)] = mtime
                except OSError:
                    pass
    return wanted


def populate(days=None, ignored_dirs=None):
    """Mirror the in-window subset of /source into /library.

    /library is staged via hardlinks (falling back to copies) so the working
    set is never physically duplicated on the NAS. seaf-cli then syncs /library.
    """
    ignored_dirs = _ignored_dir_names() if ignored_dirs is None else set(ignored_dirs)
    wanted = scan_source(days, ignored_dirs)
    log.info(
        'Ingest window: %s days — %d qualifying files; ignored dirs: %s',
        days or 'all',
        len(wanted),
        ', '.join(sorted(ignored_dirs)) or 'none',
    )

    # Remove entries from library that are no longer wanted
    for root, _dirs, files in os.walk(LIBRARY, topdown=False):
        rp = Path(root)
        for fname in files:
            lp = rp / fname
            if lp.relative_to(LIBRARY) not in wanted:
                lp.unlink(missing_ok=True)
        if rp != LIBRARY:
            try:
                rp.rmdir()
            except OSError:
                pass

    # Stage new or updated files. A hardlink shares the source inode, so its
    # mtime equals the source mtime — already-current files are skipped for free
    # on every refresh. A source file replaced in place carries a newer mtime
    # than its stale link, so it is re-linked.
    use_links = _same_filesystem(SOURCE, LIBRARY)
    placed = 0
    for rel, src_mtime in wanted.items():
        src = SOURCE / rel
        dst = LIBRARY / rel
        try:
            if dst.exists() and dst.stat().st_mtime >= src_mtime:
                continue
            dst.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
            if dst.exists():
                dst.unlink()
            _place(src, dst, use_links)
            placed += 1
        except OSError as e:
            log.warning('Skip %s: %s', rel, e)

    method = 'hardlink' if use_links else 'copy'
    log.info('Library ready — %d files staged via %s', placed, method)
    try:
        Path('/tmp/ingest-status.json').write_text(json.dumps({
            "files": len(wanted),
            "changed_files": placed,
            "ingest_days": days,
            "method": method,
            "source_path": str(SOURCE),
            "source_label": os.environ.get("SEAF_SOURCE_PATH", str(SOURCE)),
            "staging_path": str(LIBRARY),
            "ignored_dirs": sorted(ignored_dirs),
            "last_ingest_at": datetime.datetime.utcnow().isoformat() + "Z",
        }))
    except OSError:
        pass


def refresh_loop(env_days, settings_url, library_uuid):
    # subprocess.run (not os.execv) keeps this process alive so the thread survives
    def _run():
        while True:
            time.sleep(3600)
            log.info('Hourly refresh — re-populating library')
            days = resolve_ingest_days(env_days, settings_url, library_uuid)
            populate(days)
    threading.Thread(target=_run, daemon=True).start()


if __name__ == '__main__':
    raw = os.environ.get('SEAF_INGEST_DAYS', '').strip()
    env_days = int(raw) if raw else None
    log.info('SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=%s', env_days if env_days else 'not set (all files)')

    settings_url = os.environ.get('SEAF_SETTINGS_URL', '').strip() or None
    library_uuid = os.environ.get('SEAF_LIBRARY', '').strip() or None
    if settings_url:
        log.info('SEAF_SETTINGS_URL=%s', settings_url)

    days = resolve_ingest_days(env_days, settings_url, library_uuid)
    populate(days)
    refresh_loop(env_days, settings_url, library_uuid)
    # Use subprocess.run instead of os.execv so the refresh_loop thread above
    # keeps running hourly. os.execv would replace this process and kill the thread.
    result = subprocess.run([sys.executable, UPSTREAM])
    sys.exit(result.returncode)

content.text:
Now I have the full picture. Clear answer on metadata:

---

**Metadata files — the full picture:**

**1. No metadata folder inside your NAS data.** seaf-cli stores everything — its database, block cache, PID file, socket — in the Docker volume mounted at `/seafile`. Your NAS folders stay completely clean of Seafile internals.

**2. `.seafile-ignore` files.** This is a file *we create* in the root of each NAS shared folder to tell seaf-cli what to skip (like Synology's `@eaDir` thumbnail folders, `#recycle`, etc.). seaf-cli reads it but never writes it. We'd put one in each folder before the first sync.

**3. Conflict files — this is the one real thing.** If two people edit the same file at exactly the same time (one on the NAS in NYC, one in Seafile in Brazil), Seafile writes a file like this into the NAS folder:

```
Disney Logo.ai                              ← original (one version)
Disney Logo (SFConflict albert 2026-06-10-15-30).ai   ← other version
```

No data is ever lost. Someone has to manually compare and delete the one they don't want. This is identical behavior to Dropbox, Google Drive, or any 2-way sync tool — it is the only safe thing to do. It is strictly better than what Synology Drive was doing (silently reverting or losing files). In practice with design files, conflicts are rare because people don't usually open the same .psd simultaneously.

---

**The bigger thing you need to know before we proceed:**

The current architecture — the staging layer, hourly cycle, read-only mounts — was built for one-way upload and is the wrong design for what you actually need. To get real-time 2-way sync we need to:

1. Remove the `seaf-entrypoint.py` staging wrapper entirely
2. Mount the NAS folders read-write directly as seaf-cli's sync target (no `/source`, no `/library`)
3. Let seaf-cli run as a continuous daemon — it already does real-time bidirectional sync natively via inotify

The entrypoint.py (the seaf-cli daemon manager) stays. The staging layer goes away.

**One practical question before I start:** the initial sync of ~476K files in Character Licensed will take several hours — seaf-cli has to hash and upload everything it hasn't seen before. During that time the library is in a partially-synced state. Is that OK to let run overnight, or do you want to schedule it for a specific time?

When it starts against the NAS folder, it hashes local files and checks what the server already has — since those files are already in Seafile, it finds the blocks are there and skips uploading them. The "initial sync" is mostly CPU hashing time, not network transfer.

class Client:

    def __init__(self) -> None:
        self.username: str = get_configuration("SEAF_USERNAME")
        self.url: str = get_configuration("SEAF_SERVER_URL")
        self.skip_ssl_cert: bool = bool(get_configuration("SEAF_SKIP_SSL_CERT", None))
        self.upload_limit: int = get_configuration("SEAF_UPLOAD_LIMIT", None)
        self.download_limit: int = get_configuration("SEAF_DOWNLOAD_LIMIT", None)
        self.mfa_secret: str = get_configuration("SEAF_2FA_SECRET", None)

        self.password: str = get_configuration("SEAF_PASSWORD", None)
        self.token: str = get_configuration("SEAF_TOKEN", None)
        if not (self.password or self.token):
            raise BadConfiguration(
                "At least one of SEAF_PASSWORD or SEAF_TOKEN are required."
            )

        self.ini = Path.home().joinpath(".ccnet", "seafile.ini")
        self.log = Path.home().joinpath(".ccnet", "logs", "seafile.log")
        self.seafile = Path("/seafile")
        self.socket = self.seafile.joinpath("seafile-data", "seafile.sock")
        self.target = Path("/library")
        self.source = Path("/source")
        self.folder_size_cache_path = self.seafile / "folder-size-cache.json"
        self._folder_size_scan_lock = threading.Lock()
        self._folder_size_scan_running = False

        if self.socket.exists():
            self.rpc = seafile.RpcClient(str(self.socket))

# Synology NAS — Seafile seaf-cli real-time sync container
#
# One container syncs all libraries bidirectionally in real-time.
# Each NAS folder is bind-mounted read-write at /library/<name>.
# seaf-cli watches for changes via inotify and syncs within seconds.
#
# SEAF_LIBRARY_<KEY>  Library UUID for that key.
#   Key must be a single word (no underscores) — it becomes the subfolder
#   under /library and must match the bind-mount target exactly.
#
# SEAF_UPLOAD_LIMIT    Max upload speed in KB/s (optional).
# SEAF_DOWNLOAD_LIMIT  Max download speed in KB/s (optional).
#
# Image: ghcr.io/u2giants/seafile:seaf-cli-latest
# Container lives on edgesynology1.

name: seaf-cli

    cpu_shares: 512
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2g
    volumes:

      - SEAF_STATUS_TOKEN

# Synology NAS — Seafile seaf-cli upload containers
# One service per library. Each stages a date-filtered view of a NAS folder
# into /library, then seaf-cli uploads /library to the Seafile server.
#
# === Settings ===
#
# SEAF_INGEST_DAYS     Only upload files modified within this many days.
#                      Set per-library in the environment blocks below.
#                      Remove the line (or leave blank) to upload all files.
#
#   Examples:
#     - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=365    # 1 year
#     - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=730    # 2 years
#     - SEAF_INGEST_DAYS=1825   # 5 years
#
# SEAF_UPLOAD_LIMIT    Max upload speed in KB/s (optional).
# SEAF_DOWNLOAD_LIMIT  Max download speed in KB/s (optional).
#   These limit network throughput only, not the CPU hashing phase.
#   Example: 10240 = 10 MB/s
#
# Resource limits:
#   cpu_shares — soft CPU weight (default 1024; 512 = half priority under contention).
#                NOTE: Synology kernel does not support CFS CPU quota (hard cpus limit).
#                cpu_shares only throttles when the NAS is CPU-saturated, not always.
#   memory     — hard limit; OOMKill if exceeded (container restarts cleanly).

content.text:
Pushed. Here's what changed and what you need to do:

---

**What changed:**
- **Single container** — one `seaf-cli` container handles all 3 libraries instead of one per folder
- **Real-time 2-way sync** — seaf-cli's daemon watches for file changes via inotify and syncs within seconds in both directions
- **NAS folders mounted read-write** — changes from designers in Brazil come straight back to the NAS in NYC
- **`.seafile-ignore` auto-created** — on first start, the container writes an ignore file in each NAS folder so Synology junk (`@eaDir`, `#recycle`, etc.) never gets uploaded to Seafile

**What you need to do on the NAS:**

The old containers (`seaf-cli-char-licensed`, `seaf-cli-generic-decor`) need to be stopped and removed, and the new single `seaf-cli` container started. In Container Manager on the NAS, or via SSH:

```bash
# Stop and remove old containers
docker rm -f seaf-cli-char-licensed seaf-cli-generic-decor seaf-cli-styleguides

# Pull latest image and start new single container
cd /path/to/synology-seaf-cli
docker compose pull
docker compose up -d
```

The old data volumes (`seaf-cli-char-licensed-data`, etc.) can be deleted after the new container is running cleanly — they're no longer used.

**On first start:** seaf-cli will hash the ~530K files across all three libraries and compare against what's already in Seafile. No re-uploading, just indexing. It'll log when each library is "already synced" or starts syncing. After that, changes are near-instant in both directions.

content.text:
Yes, it's working. The logs show exactly what we want:

- `seaf-cli init` — fresh setup done ✓
- `seaf-daemon` started ✓  
- `.seafile-ignore` written to both NAS folders ✓
- `Starting to download...` — this is the initial reconciliation: seaf-cli is comparing the Seafile library contents against the NAS folders. Since the seaf-cli data volume is brand new (no index), it needs to verify which files are already there. Files that match won't be re-downloaded.

Let it run for 10–15 minutes, then check logs again to see how the sync is progressing:

```bash
sudo /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker logs seaf-cli --tail 50
```

You can also watch it live:

```bash
sudo /var/packages/ContainerManager/target/usr/bin/docker logs seaf-cli -f
```

Once the initial reconciliation finishes, any file saved on the NAS will appear in Seafile within seconds, and any file a designer saves in Seafile will appear on the NAS within seconds.
